entry.title: App Server in GCP vs Legacy for Incentive Management
entry.content:
I need to talk with someone who knows the GCP architecture for Incentive Management and can help me connect to the App Server in GCP vs how it's done today in Legacy (Oracle).

Currently, we're running a script in Legacy that checks 2 folders in the App Server for files and if any are found, inserts data into an Oracle table that is used by Advanced Workflow.

We would like to do the same thing in GCP but need some guidance on how to connect to the App Server in that environment.

Please let me know if you need any further information.

Thanks in advance!

Cheers!

Ron

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano 

My name is Odion, and I am an accountant based in Nigeria with a deep passion for leveraging technology to drive business efficiency. I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the SAP Digital Skills Program

I hold a Bachelor's degree in Accounting from The University of Lagos and over the past 4 years, I have gained extensive experience working in various accounting roles. My professional journey has equipped me with a robust understanding of financial management, reporting, and compliance. However, I recognize the growing importance of digital transformation in the finance sector and am eager to expand my expertise in this area.

The SAP Digital Skills Program presents a remarkable opportunity for me to acquire cutting-edge skills in SAP software, which I believe is essential for driving innovation and efficiency in today's dynamic business environment. My strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and commitment to continuous learning make me a good fit for this program. I am particularly excited about the prospect of integrating SAP's powerful tools into my workflow to enhance financial processes and decision-making.

I am confident that participating in this program will not only bolster my technical capabilities but also enable me to contribute more effectively to my organization and the broader accounting community.

Thank you for considering my application. I am looking forward to the possibility of joining this transformative program and contributing to its success.

entry.title: Re: C_SECAUTH_20 SAP System Security and Authorizations -...
entry.content:
Hello,

here is the answer from SAP:

Die beiden Zertifizierungen (C_SECAUTH_20, P_SECAUTH_21), wurden mittlerweile retired und es gibt auch keine Ersatz- bzw. Nachfolge Zertifizierung dafür.

but if i click the add to cart the ID should show in the MessageBox or Alert but its not working

Alert result

entry.title: Re: SAP C_SECAUTH_20 Certification
entry.content:
Hello,

here is the answer from SAP:

Die beiden Zertifizierungen (C_SECAUTH_20, P_SECAUTH_21), wurden mittlerweile retired und es gibt auch keine Ersatz- bzw. Nachfolge Zertifizierung dafür.

entry.title: Re: How to Setup Direct connection from your On Prem to SAP Logistics Business Network Freight Coll
entry.content:
HI

Thanks for the blog

My architecture is S/4 HANA 2023 FPS1 On premise, SAP Cloud Connector, SAP BTP, IAS, with LBN Shipper and Carrier.

I followed the blog to create certificate in the IAS, added in my S/4 HANA STRUST, uploaded also certificate from url https://l20398-iflmap.hcisbp.eu1.hana.ondemand.com

But in my SOAMANGER, i always have an error 401

I don't arrive to understand whats wrong

entry.title: Re: Uploading Customer Hierarchy - VDH1N Transaction
entry.content:
Hi together,

This post is quite old, but I had the same problem recently. I built a migration project with this BAPI and had the error in the migration cockpit when migrating the customer hierarchies that customer xy was not created, although it was. The leading zeros were missing. The number field for customers has ten digits. This means that not only the customer number e.g. 1234 has to be entered but also 0000001234. There are no conversion routines here.

entry.title: How to Join Composition Data in a CDS View for Reporting in SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm working on an SAP CAP project and need some assistance with a specific reporting requirement. I have an entity called Invoice that has a composition to another entity InvoiceLines. I'm trying to create a reporting view that includes data from both Invoice and its composition InvoiceLines, but I'm facing issues with the nodeInvoiceLines data not appearing in my reporting view.

Here is a simplified version of my current CDS model:

 

context InvoiceObject {

    entity Invoice : managed, cuid {
        key invoiceCode       : String(20) @(Core.Computed: true) @readonly;
        status                : Association to one InvoiceStatus @readonly;
        type                  : Association to one InvoiceType @mandatory;
        company               : Association to one RECompanies @mandatory;
        date                  : Date @mandatory;
        customer              : Association to one BusinessPartner @mandatory;
        billCustomer          : Association to one BusinessPartner;
        billAddress           : Association to one BillingAddressNode;
        externalReference     : String(50);
        currency              : Currency @mandatory;
        netAmount             : Amount @readonly;
        grossAmount           : Amount @readonly;
        taxAmount             : Amount @readonly;
        retentionAmount       : Amount @readonly;
        salesUnit             : Association to one SalesUnit @mandatory;
        invoiceUnit           : Association to one InvoiceUnit @mandatory;
        externalSystem        : String(40);
        nodeInvoiceLines      : Composition of many InvoiceLines on nodeInvoiceLines.invoice = $self;
        month                 : String;
    }

    entity InvoiceStatus : CodeList {
        key code : String(1);
    }

    entity InvoiceType : CodeList {
        key code : Integer;
    }

    entity InvoiceLines : managed, cuid {
        line            : Integer @readonly;
        service         : Association to one Services @mandatory;
        description     : String(100);
        netAmount       : Amount;
        grossAmount     : Amount @readonly;
        taxAmount       : Amount @readonly;
        tax             : Association to one cfg.TaxCodes @mandatory;
        retention       : Decimal(5, 2) @assert.range : [0, 100] @Measures.Unit: '%';
        retentionAmount : Amount @readonly;
        invoice         : Association to one Invoice;
        site            : Association to one obj.SitesObject.Sites;
        building        : Association to one obj.BuildingObjects.Buildings;
        unit            : Association to one obj.UnitObjects.Units;
        contract        : Association to one obj.ContractObject.Contract;
    }

    @readonly
    entity Reporting as select from Invoice {
        invoiceCode,
        status,
        type,
        company,
        date,
        customer,
        billCustomer,
        billAddress,
        externalReference,
        currency,
        netAmount,
        grossAmount,
        taxAmount,
        retentionAmount,
        salesUnit,
        invoiceUnit,
        externalSystem,
        month // Include the computed month
    };
}

 

However, when I query the Reporting entity, the nodeInvoiceLines data does not appear in the results.

What I've Tried:

  • Ensuring that nodeInvoiceLines is properly defined as a composition in the Invoice entity.
  • Using the expand keyword to explicitly include the nodeInvoiceLines data in the Reporting view.

Questions:

  1. How can I join the Invoice and InvoiceLines entities in my CDS view to ensure that nodeInvoiceLines data is included in the Reporting view?
  2. Are there best practices for joining data from an entity and its composition in a CDS view?

Any advice or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Resolving Session Persistence Issues in OData Services
entry.content:
Hello,

I am not sure if it will solve your issue but did you called method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_ODATA_MODEL~SET_SOFT_STATE_ENABLED of your model in the MPC_EXT class ?

Regards

entry.title: Re: ODATA API - Condition Record for Pricing in Sales Tax Code
entry.content:
Hello,

After 3 years since this question, the tax code still missing in API API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV/ ?

The link you shared doesn't work.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C - Knowledgebase Mashup
entry.content:
Hello,

i was having issues with integrating a knowledge Base into C4C, the mashup could not be opened in Mashup Authoring after its creation and it also did not work in the ticket (Solution Finder Tab), an error was thrown.

In my case the issue was the language, in the customers tenant, everything was maintained in german language but apparently the KNOWLEDGE_BASE Mashup (and its webservice as well ?) needs to be created in an english login. After i created it in an english login, the mashup worked right away.

I hope this helps someone with the same problem.

Greetings

Lars Wiete

entry.title: New e-learning to help understand how SAP Enterprise Automation can improve your business processes
entry.content:
To automate processes from end to end, organizations need to integrate across their IT landscapes, prioritize critical automations, utilize benchmarks to quantify success and measure progress, and orchestrate workflows across multiple applications. SAP offers a unique and comprehensive AI-powered solution to integrate applications, discover process inefficiencies, and automate processes across heterogeneous enterprise environments. This is called Enterprise Automation.

We have recently released a new e-learning Understand how SAP Enterprise Automation can improve your business processes which gives an overview to Enterprise Automation and discuss how SAP delivers Enterprise Automation. Also, we introduce the business process to digitize non-PO invoice document processing with Enterprise Automation which will be used during the e-learning to show case the capabilities of the solutions working together.

Please go here for a direct link to the e-learning. Before you can access the registration links, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

BTP Lead – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: is checking at line item level in S4HANA tcode VA05 is new feature?
entry.content:
is checking at line item level in S4HANA tcode VA05 is new feature?
appreciate if someone can guide me on which SAP notes / documentation that explains this improved features?
it wasnt the case in ECC.

p/s: i've tried scouring for SAP Notes but none relevant to what i am looking for

Thanks!
Azizul

entry.title: What attribute should I use to represent FioriAppID for "Task" in Signavio
entry.content:
Hi. 

I would appreciate it if you could answer following question.

I downloaded a BPMN file from SAP Best Practice(for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Procurement of Direct Materials (J45))

and imported the file(Import BPMN 2.0 xml) to Signavio(30 days trial edition).

I found that Fiori App ID("F0842A") is not mapped in any attributes.

We can see the App ID and App name in "Test Script" but these are not imported to Signavio by BPMN file.

 

[Question]

Which attribute should I use to represent FioriAppID( or TCODE) for "Task" in Signavio process manager?

If there's no standard attribute, do we have to create own custom attribute for it?

 

[What I've checked]

I've investigated HELP Portal but according to following page, there's no specific attribute for FioriAppID.

https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/workspace-admin-guide/main-attributes-and-more-attributes-for-diagram-types?locale=en-US&q=attribute

entry.title: how can we display structure of the functional location and equipments in SAP asset management ?
entry.content:
Hello,

i have a business need, the requirment is to find a way to display all functional locations and equipment in a structural way like the one in the transaction IH01 in SAP S4HANA on premise as shown in the screenshot  below : 

is there any app we could use on SAP public cloud ?

 

thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Odata Update/PUT method break point not Triggering
entry.content:
Well It worked like charm. Thanks alot. However, would you kindly help with how did you analyzed?

The payload was generated on copying from right hand panel using Copy button.

entry.title: Balance Carry forward(FAGLGVTR) runs for several years
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am in a project to Upgrade S4 1511 to S4/han 2023. I executed the FAGLGVTR for 2024, when reviewing the ACDOCA table it was executed from 2018 to 2024, placing initial balances in each year in period 0, but the execution What I did was only in 2024.

entry.title: Re: Given a date, how to find the nearest Monday using SA...
entry.content: Hi @SP9 , I´m afraid this can´t be solved with a data action. If it´s an option for you, it´s more likely you can solve it with scripting in a story. -- Thomas

entry.title: Fiori Elements Flexible Programming Model Variant Management does not offer 'Apply Automatically'
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a new custom page based Fiori Elements app ( deployed on ABAP Cloud ) which 'mimics' the standard list page.  The reason being that I need some custom elements in the dynamic page header and extensions for this are only supported on the object page - not the list page ( way to go SAP ...  sigh).

Anyways, got everything working fine .. I have a smart filterbar and table linked with a variant management control type

<v:VariantManagement id="vm" for="FilterBar, StatisticsTable" showSetAsDefault="true" executeOnSelectionForStandardDefault="true"/>

Custom variant views can be saved BUT what I am missing is the checkbox option 'Apply Automatically'.

The 'standard' mobile smart variant control has a property called 'supportApplyAutomatically' which allows the setting (at least based on the doc - not verified myself ).

I could not vind any similar property on the 'sap.ui.fl.variants.VariantManagement' control to actually configure the behavior. I tried all existing properties but no luck. 

Thanks,

Steven

entry.title: Re: Generate Intercompany Billing Request in SAP On-Premise System

entry.title: Re: Generate Intercompany Billing Request in SAP On-Premi...
entry.content:
Hi,
I have question to what you have shared. Is it possible to use BRF+ to trigger idoc for Supplier Invoice creation when Intercompany Invoice is posted?

entry.title: CAL - Waiting for Export Control
entry.content:
For the last few days I have been trying to create a CAL Appliance of the latest versions of S4HANA. I have been getting this popup:

"Your request is under clarification by Export Control..."

I have been waiting a week, but I received no email from SAP. What can I do?

Praful

SAP Cloud Appliance Library

entry.title: Re: Issue with "Page down" and "Column width optimize" us...
entry.content:
Create a tiny executable program to see if the code in your big program is the culprit.

As you can see here, there is no problem (NB: the problem is not about the container where you display the ALV grid):

REPORT ztest.
PARAMETERS dummy.

DATA gt_asn_final TYPE TABLE OF T100.
DATA o_grid_asn TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF o_grid_asn IS NOT BOUND.
    SELECT * FROM T100 UP TO 100 ROWS INTO TABLE @GT_asn_final.
    DATA(gt_fcat) = VALUE lvc_t_fcat( ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'LVC_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE'
      EXPORTING
        i_structure_name       = 'T100'
      CHANGING
        ct_fieldcat            = gt_fcat
      EXCEPTIONS
        inconsistent_interface = 1
        program_error          = 2
        OTHERS                 = 3.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    ENDIF.
    o_grid_asn = NEW cl_gui_alv_grid( i_parent = cl_gui_container=>screen0 ).
    o_grid_asn->set_table_for_first_display(
        EXPORTING
          is_layout       = VALUE #( cwidth_opt = 'X'
                                     edit       = 'X' )
          i_save          = 'A'
        CHANGING
          it_outtab       = gt_asn_final
          it_fieldcatalog = gt_fcat[] ).
  ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Connecting to an SAP Analytics Cloud tenant to SAP Da...
entry.content:
Hello Sebastian,

We did open an incident, and they said the error was indeed due to our tenants being in different landscapes.

In our case they're in US and BR, and connection between tenants in different regions is not supported ☹️.

We also asked if the roadmap included this, but they said the current versión doesn't have it, they said we could create an enhancement request for this (3332382 - How to create an enhancement request for SAP Datasphere? - SAP for Me)

entry.title: Re: FM/BAPI to update sales price in IS-Retail
entry.content: BAPI_PRICECATALOGUE_SAVREPLICA,

entry.title: /SCWM/PI_CC_CREATE result to be one Physical Inventory Document containing separate WO
entry.content: When executing /SCWM/PI_CC_CREATE the business would like the end result to be one Physical Inventory Document containing separate Warehouse Orders with only one Product in each Warehouse Order. SAP support will not assist so wondering if anyone has experience with the same

entry.title: Re: co products---equivalence number
entry.content:
Hello
Whenever you are using co-products, you should go to the material master tab MRP 2 and click the button JOINT PRODUCTION, where you will be able to maintain an apportionment structure with the equivalence numbers.
The percentage that you maintain here is a portion of the order costs that will be settled to the co-product, so you need to discuss with your business users and costing team what is the percentage that should be used.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Process order showing double component in materials tab.
entry.content:
Hello

It looks like the kind of issue created by custom code. Are you sure you don't have any BAdI or user-exit implemented in the production order or in CIF?

Regards
Caetano

entry.title: Certificate based authentication in MDK client
entry.content:
I enabled the certificate based authentication and configure the branded settings as well. Build MDK client app and install it on my android device successfully now the MDK Client must detects a valid certificate installed on the device and connects to the SAP BTP but no certificate pop up appears on the screen and the app navigated to Login page. I followed the tutorial for building custom mdk client and completed all the steps but not sure about the certificate. Do i need to install a certificate to android device or the client will fetch existing certificate. I’ll appreciate the help here if someone has any solution for this please share. I already have spent 3 days in finding the solution but no luck so far.

 

SAP BTP SDK for Android

entry.title: Change output when "delivery completed" indicator ( ELIKZ) is set in background
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a requirement to trigger change output when delivery completed indicator is set/unchecked in background. Can you please suggest how it can be achieved?

We found one SAP NOTE 1987986 which helps in creating the change log but it is unclear if it will work when GR is posted via eWM or via BAPI or API.

Thanks in advance for sharing your feedback.

Best Regards

Rajan

entry.title: Pushing Value with wrong format in Excel
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I used this Custom Script to get the string '2.730' from ' 2.730 K'

this work's 

But if I push this value to Excel it ends in '2,73' (the number format of the cell is standard then)

What can I do so that the bot writes the correct string to Excel? Can I specify the format or something similar?
It would be very important that the '2.730' is in the Excel at the end 

Can anyone help me? Many thanks in advance.
And best regards

Paul

entry.title: Re: Certificate based authentication in MDK client
entry.content:
@RKJ Can you verify if you have configured the settings in mobile services admin UI and in BrandedSettings.json for your MDK client as per this tutorial?

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-mobile-dev-kit-cert-auth.html

entry.title: Re: Screen Painter SE51 dump CALLBACK_REJECTED_BY_WHITELIST
entry.content:
Hello,

Please be careful when copying the callback allowlist value pairs from this blog in the corresponding fields of the callback positive list of the RFC destination EU_SCRP_WN32:
- The entry with callback FM "RS_SCRP_GF_PROCESS_640RFC_GET" is wrong and should not be copied.
- Special signs and/or leading spaces could be copied from the browser into the callback positive list of the RFC destination and thus the runtime error will persist (see note 3378219/KBA KB0704776)

If you encounter the runtime error CALLBACK_REJECTED_BY_WHITELIST in graphical Screen Painter, you should mainly stick to the official note 2251931 which contains all the necessary information.

entry.title: 500 missing Zone Information and internal server error while launching the deployed HTML5 build ap
entry.content:

Please check and help me to resolve the issue.

Regards,

Rakesh

entry.title: Re: Certificate based authentication in MDK client
entry.content: @Jitendra_Kansal Yes, I followed the same tutorial step by step and configured oAuth in mobile service and updated the BrandedSettings.json as well. Build through Cloud build and Local machine but results are same.

entry.title: Deleting Shopping Cart in SAP Business By Design
entry.content:
How to Delete Shopping Cart which is already Requested?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Z-tcode creation for CJ8G
entry.content: Please change your tag to ABAP Development. also, refer to this previous posting: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/z-tcode-for-standard-sap-program/m-p/3970929

entry.title: Re: Deleting Shopping Cart in SAP Business By Design
entry.content:
Shopping cart can be deleted only if it is in "in preparation" status. Once it is requested, follow up purchase request will be created from the cart and so system will not allow you to delete the cart anymore.

You can cancel the shopping cart by cancelling the remaining open quantity of the purchase request created as a follow-on document of the shopping cart.

If there are follow up delivery documents and purchase orders, please cancel them in reverse order, i.e. cancel the delivery document first, then cancel the purchase order and then cancel the open quantity in purchase request at item level using Actions-> Cancel Open Quantity.

This will cancel the shopping cart.

If the purchase order is in preparation status, you can delete it and then cancel open quantity of purchase request.

entry.title: Mutiple Warning message for field check
entry.content:
Hello All,

Reference to subjected issue we need your expertise help, for our client at outbound delivery we already have implemented custom logic for check custom field and selected as an "I' information if field is missing before saving the delivery at both create and change mode and it's working perfectly fine.

For fulfill our client requirement we need to implement one more custom logic in same outbound delivery business Badi but after implementing we are able see only one error message at one time. if both fields also missing system showing only one error.

So is there any code logic available so we able see both missing field error and warning message same time that we implemented in business logic  

Please share your input it will really be helpful for us. 

Thanks 

Samadhan

entry.title: Re: BPFCTRA0 message 00 208 “Inconsistent assigned values have been found”
entry.content: Please submit a ticket to SAP Support.

entry.title: Sapphire 2024: Make the Connections That Make the Most of Your Supply Chain
entry.content:
Bring Out Your Best.  A simple statement that holds a powerful truth to it, one that is the pivotal theme to this year’s SAP Sapphire and ASUG Conference.  When people think of their best, what comes to mind?  Perhaps personal success in the form of accolades and rewards; to others, reaching a destination at the end of their journey.  For us here in SAP Supply Chain Management, we want one thing: to break down silos!

Whether we realize it or not, supply chain impacts an entire company, and efficient processes cannot be run through siloed processes with expected success. However, good news – there’s something companies can do about it.

Find out how SAP breaks down those walls to turn supply chains into a strategic, sustainable asset and what you can expect to learn and experience at the Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference.

Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Drive Business Innovation

Over the last year, Generative AI and AI have been the hottest topic in board rooms across the world, sparking conversations on the latest innovations and technologies that companies can utilize to promote efficiency and drive out complacency.   AI’s relevancy in supply chain is growing daily, addressing challenges from design to operate, as it helps augment the workforce in warehouses and factories through data-driven tools, empowering people within the supply chain to make more informed and timely decisions.

Technologies, as such, have the power to be a transformative force in organizations’ supply chains.  In its utilization, companies can develop smarter assets and products, driving out process inefficiencies, and connecting real-time information to their equipment and products, allowing for innovative business models.  Attendees can now see this technology in action at Sapphire 2024 at these enticing sessions:

SAP and Google Cloud Technology Accelerates AI-Driven Supply Chains

Navigating Today’s Logistics World with Data and AI

Supercharge Field Service Management Operations with AI

I’m Still Standing: Resilience Built into Your Supply Chain

Expect the unexpected has been a statement that’s rung true through supply chains globally. Disruptions can come in many forms – from geopolitical unrest to a surge in demand – both qualifying as a disturbance to your processes. Companies now need to have the ability to not only sense, identify, or predict these disruptions, but also have the visibility built in to adapt to them, as well. Understanding and pinpointing critical areas of risk and opportunity is key to an agile supply chain, as building resiliency into business processes allows companies to stand the test of time.

Companies now have the ability to see disruptions before they happen and have the systems in place to not only sense it but respond to it.  One of the main ways to drive resiliency throughout your supply chain is having those foundational interconnections between those systems, especially between the functional areas of your business.   Interoperability throughout your ecosystem is key, and Sapphire attendees have the chance to learn more at these exciting sessions:

Expand Your Business with a Risk-Resilient and AI-Enabled Supply Chain

Enhancing Supply Chain Resilience with AI and Freight Visibility

Achieve Supply Chain Resiliency Through Planning Excellence

Let’s Talk: Interoperability Across Your Systems

Scalability at large. Rapid deployment of innovation. Ease of adoption. One may question the connection between these three, and they all come to one word: Cloud. SAP’s Cloud ERP allows companies to work in an interoperable system, letting them work across end-to-end departments and solutions to manage cross-functional business processes. In a sense, when organizations successfully integrate the entire supply chain process – from design, planning, and manufacturing to logistics, maintenance, and service – and then integrate that into the rest of the business via native, in-depth ERP, they can drive differentiation.

In today’s digital age, why is this important?

It allows companies to run as an intelligent enterprise, which, in turns, connects the processes that promote better contextualized decisions.  These vital decisions are not only based on data from the business side, but in conjunction with the organizations’ operational side, as well.  This holistic approach promotes internal connectivity, opening the door for a seamless interconnected ecosystem, where organizations can transform the information without complex systems and multiple providers.   Those attending Sapphire can learn more about driving interoperability in a Cloud based environment at these innovative sessions:

Revolutionizing Supply Chains Using RISE with SAP at BBC

Unleash Your Potential with Enterprise-Wide Adoption of Cloud Solutions

Embarking on a Digital Manufacturing Transformation at King’s Hawaiian

The ability to keep up with the ever-evolving and growing rate of change – for instance, sustainability mandates and regulations – plus leveraging Business AI in the Cloud – in essence, having the embedded technology across all your systems and solutions to leverage real-time data and cutting-edge innovation to grow at scale – can not only change the way companies do business, but allow them to bring out their best.

Companies, organizations, executives, and attendees alike now have the chance to learn more about the cutting-edge technology that’s SAP is bringing to offer.  From AI to resiliency to interoperability, companies can better navigate the challenges they face and rapidly adopt to the evolving digital business environment.  At SAP Sapphire, you’ll see how you build a supply chain that will stand the test of time, shift to business-changing transformation, embed cutting-edge innovation across your departments, and, at the core, bring out your best.

We hope to see you in Orlando at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference.  Click here to register.

entry.title: EDIFACT PRIDES message not available in type system
entry.content:
We have requirement to map to Finnish Edifact message PRIDES

UNH+1+PRIDES:D:00A:UN+FI0093'

This is a DESADV extended with segments like MOA, PRI and TAX

This is not available in EDIFACT Type System. Any way to add this in order to use TPM B2B solution in SAP Cloud Integration?

Regards Brian

entry.title: Re: RAP: Activate Draft and trigger Save on custom action...
entry.content: I've the same issue. Were you able to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Campaign auto restart after error - adjust timing
entry.content:
Hello @vervinckt_joyca,
Long time! Hope all good at your end 🙂
I think you can check on the T-Code SM39 and can find the program name which retriggers after 30 minutes and I believe you can also check the SM37 for programs and you can maybe tweak the time by a custom code to 'check any program' which are awaiting for 30 minutes to run less than that.
I cannot guarantee the solution as yMkt OnPrem is no longer supported by SAP.

I hope this answers your query.

Best Regards,
Kunal Bansal | SAP CX Solution Architect
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Re: How to create a table in SAP HANA Cloud Database with...
entry.content: I don't myself have not myself used Fiori Elements but a query to a JSON collection can either return the JSON or if you specify the column names, it will return a tabular result.

entry.title: Problem with One SU created from multiple at Goods Receipt
entry.content: Hi, I have received one material which consisted of a number of SUs. The batch is the same for all  the material, and it is being stored in a bulk storage area which is a push back bin. The problem is that the new SU numbers  are not being created. One SU keeps getting bigger. For example 1 SU should be 1000 ea only but  now one SU is increasing quantity each time I receive another  and now has 4000 ea. No new SUs number are being generated. Anyone have any idea why this could be?  How can i stop this happening?

entry.title: Single Invoice per Payer and different Sold to
entry.content:
Hi all,

My business requirement is to have the possibility to create a single invoice with the same Payer and different Sold-To party without an invoice list.

I need only the possibility, it means that when users required create a single invoice, is not a must.

Any idea?

Please suggest me any solutions!

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry - Clearing (Asynchronous) API has BUG
entry.content:
Hi Abu_Monk,

I hope you are doing well,

Requesting you to please tell us the process in step-by-step to overcome, kindly share sample request payload to post for clearing payment.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: UUIDs and Unique Semantic Primary Keys
entry.content:
Well, it looks like there is no easier way to do that. I tried to create a unique index in the table, but that only caused a dump and it is not handled by the framework and frontend. Additionally, there is no way to catch that problem in the RAP behavior class and transform it into a nice message (at least not in managed mode).

So my solution was to create a validation for checking the uniqueness of the Semantic Key.

I'll report my solution here for those who come after me:

  1. Added a validation in the behavior.

  • Selected the data in the method created in the Behavior class and checked for uniqueness, returning a reported and failed message. Make sure to exclude the record we're creating/editing (uuid <> actual uuid).

Hope was helpfull for someone.

Cheers

ps: Thank you for the feedback @peterpersiel

entry.title: Re: How to migrate SAP BO reports to Tableau?
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Re: how can we display structure of the functional locati...
entry.content:
Hi @Hichem_Rahmani 

 

The nearest we have to IH01 in S/4HANA Cloud is the Asset Viewer 

 

  • Display the technical object in the Asset Viewer.

    You can display the hierarchical structure of the object and navigate to subordinate objects, such as materials. In addition, you can open the usage list and view the history of the installation location of a piece of equipment which is installed in a technical object. The system records a usage period for each installation location, enabling you to track the complete installation history.

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: how can we display structure of the functional locati...
Validity of CSRF Token
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

When using the POST commands to the BP API (or any other API) we see that the CSRF token doesn't seem to expire. I can't find any documentation on how long the CSRF token should stay valid. We need to know this in order to allow correct integration flows. 

Can you provide information on how long the CSRF tokens are valid in S/4 Cloud environments?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Validity of CSRF Token
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

By default the CSRF token remains valid for 24 hours (86400 seconds), however the validity is bound to the security session, which depends on the system parameter http/security_session_timeout value. In S/4HANA Cloud the http/security_session_timeout value is 30 minutes. 

The token request is for 24 hours but it is still tied to the security session as specified. So, the validity is only 30 minutes on cloud by default. 

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: SAP BODS - Data exporting in incorrect order (.csv file)
entry.content:
We have an ETL batch job that loads data from a database table into a CSV file. The first column in the CSV file is a key column. Although all the values are exported successfully, the key values are not sorted in the CSV file. 

 

#SAP BODS

#Data Services

SAP Data Services

Here is my destination,

Isyraf

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP + Fiori Elements with Error in Draft oDataV4
entry.content: Hi @gregorw ! Wow, your tip was a trigger for a well-structured world regarding Draft, I learned a lot, thank you very much! I spoke with Jodel and it seems that the new version of Node.js has impacted the Plugin solution and is no longer adherent. We continue to wait for the solution, and for now we will just leave it with the Read and Delete action to serve the customer, and looking for some alternatives, Fiori Free Style perhaps. Note: Great presentations at reCAP2023, congratulations!

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to restrict which Business Role is **bleep**...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

the feature you're asking for is available. Sub-admins can be created. Mostly for that, the concept of Role Groups and User Groups has been introduced. 3 restriction types have been introduced together with the two grouping possibilities:
Business Role (S_BRL)
Business User (CLASS)
Business Role User Assignment (S_BRL_ASG)

How to:
1) Make up your mind on how you want to group business users and/or business roles. Hint: as one role/user can only be assigned to one group, the groups should be rather small.
Examples: create rather "Controlling France" than only "France" or only "Controlling"  and then also "Sales France" and "Controlling Spain" etc.
On the restriction side you can assign multiple groups to sub admins. Looking at the previous example you could define an admin responsible for all Controlling related groups or one responsible for France etc.
You can group either roles or users or both.
2) Create Role Groups and assign all Business Roles belonging to the group to them.
3) Create User Groups and assign all Business Users belonging to the group to them.
4) Create sub admin Business Roles as needed by combining the IAM related business catalogs that are in scope.
5) Maintain the three restriction types as needed:
Business Role (S_BRL) => add all business role groups that shall be accessible/maintainable
Business User (CLASS) => add all business user groups that shall be accessible/maintainable
Business Role User Assignment (S_BRL_ASG) => with that you can restrict role assignments to allow a sub admin to only assign business roles from the mentioned group to users from the other mentioned group.

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Christian Hochwarth

entry.title: Re: Odata Update/PUT method break point not Triggering
entry.content: odata is a specification, they have specific format requirement. you need to know odata protocol

entry.title: Bill C27 and Quebec law 25 (prev. bill 64)-Employee Data Retention and Destruction in SAP
entry.content:

In view of employee personal information protection as per Bill C27 and Quebec law 25 (prev. bill 64)- how are organizations who use SAP (on premise), implementing Employee Data Retention and Destruction requirements?

As part of SAP ILM (Information Lifecycle Management), I see Data Archiving and Destruction module available. Does anyone have any input or guidance as to how this can be implemented to apply organization's data retention schedule?

entry.title: can code lists created in custom fields app be directly changed / add new values in production sys
entry.content:
Hello all, 

Using S/4 HANA 2021 (on-prem)

Custom code lists for a custom field have been created in development using Custom fields app. Code list have values and they have been transported to production system. This custom field is created under business context 'Accounting: Market Segment' and have been enabled for usage in various Reports and UIs and SOAP API.

Q1: Can the code lists values be added or changed directly in production system? 

Q2: If yes, what if there is change in the custom field code list or if it has been enabled for other reports and transported from Development system, will the new changes overwrite the code list values that have been added by key users in production as the TR from development doesn't have these values? 

Q3: If no, should the key users also do the change from development and transport? for releasing TR will they have to access the GUI/backend again?

Please skip Q3 if answer for changing or adding code lists in development .

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/key-user-custom-fields-when-to-use-what-type-of-code-list/ba-p/13557853

In the above blog - it says that code lists can be changed only from dev and transported . So please clarify me if key users can directly change existing values or add new values in production or not.

Thanks,

Archana

 

ABAP Extensibility SAP Fiori Launchpad ##S/4 HANA on-prem##S/4 HANA Extensibility,SAP in-app extensibility,SAP Key user extensibility, Custom Fields , custo

entry.title: Repository of documents in guided sourcing
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client is looking for a repository that could store template documents or any documents that is related to Sourcing and Contracts. This repository should be accessible to all users that they can access from projects. In addition, they want to have the users capable to add new documents and if possible, to have messaging functionality. 

We have explored Knowledge Projects; however, it is currently available on Classic Sourcing, but we are using Guided Sourcing. We are looking for other ways that we can store document and can be accessed in Guided Sourcing.

Hope someone could help us on this. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: May 22, 2024, Edition
entry.content:
No, your eyes do not deceive you. SAP Community Voice -- our long-time email newsletter -- is making the transition to What's New.

As announced in the May 22 edition of the newsletter, we're doing two more editions in June, then moving SAP Community Voice to What's New permanently.

To help everyone prepare for this change, we're starting to publish SAP Community Voice content to What's New now. In fact, the items here come straight from the May 22 edition of SAP Community Voice.

If you subscribe to the email newsletter, expect two more -- one on June 5 and June 19 -- before we make the permanent switch to What's New. After that, please make sure you've subscribed to What's New so you don't miss our biweekly breakdown of community highlights, news, and more!

For now, please enjoy these entries from our latest -- albeit a little late for here -- newsletter.

 

Featured Content

 

Participate in Global Running Day with Other SAP Community Members! 

We teased this event in the previous newsletter, and now we're happy to announce that one of our SAP Champions is driving a grassroots effort for Global Running Day. Runners will gather on June 5 to run the Tan in Melbourne, Australia, but you can participate virtually from June 1 to 9! 

Read the blog post

 

What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q2 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC) 

The headline says it all: This blog post highlights the latest features in the Q2 2024 release of SAP Analytics Cloud. The post includes detailed information, as well as a video demonstrating what's new in this quarterly release. Our product experts cover everything! 

Read the blog post

 

First Steps for SAP Cloud ALM Deployment Scenario for ABAP Systems 

You'll need to set aside some time for this one, but once you read this thorough post, you'll be able to create a test landscape to practice with the SAP Cloud ALM deployment scenario without creating interferences with the real landscapes for the transport management system. 

Read the blog post

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration with SAP Datasphere

This post explains how to create a connection between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Datasphere, exploring integration strategies and real-world applications to achieve operational excellence, drive innovation and efficiency, and improve decision-making. 

Read the blog post

 

Community News

 

Getting the Most from SAP Community 

Per the introduction, What's New is the place to go for the latest announcements about SAP Community -- and it's also becoming the home for the SAP Community Voice newsletter. You'll want to subscribe here to stay up to date -- and also read our help section, watch our how-to videos, and take the Tour the SAP Community 2024 tutorial. 

 

Meet Your Peers Online and In Person at SAP Community Events! 

Global Running Day, a combination of in-person and virtual activities, isn't the only upcoming event scheduled by and for our members! Visit our events section to learn about SAP CodeJam, SAP Inside Track, and SAP Stammtisch meetups, as well as other online and in-person events.

 

Show Off Your Support for SAP Community Offline 

When you log out from SAP Community, do you still want the world to see how much your community membership means to you? Then you'll want to pick up something from our online store. We offer a variety of shirts, hoodies, socks, and more -- all proudly displaying the SAP Community name, so you can look stylish and smart at the same time.

entry.title: The field Product Hierarchy (MARA-PRDHA) does not appear in transaction MM01, MM02 or MM03
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
I have requirement to do classification of MRO materials in Product Hierarchy.
For a test I have maintained the root node and sub node and assign FERT material to the PH.

 


But no material shows PRDHA field in general data not even the one for which I have assigned the PH.

I have checked config "Maintain Field Selection for Data Screens"
You can see for FERT it is optional entry

The 2nd problem is material type ERSA (Spare) is not in field ref list.

Can anyone please suggest how can I see the PRDHA fiel in general data.

entry.content:
Dear All,

During the transfer of goods from the raw material storage to the production storage location against the production order, there must be acceptance at the production storage location.

Kindly provide the possible solution

Regards,

Subramani

entry.title: SAP Profile Generation "Merge" function
entry.content:
recently I started noticing that in S/4 the Export Mode of the profile generator is behaving differently than it used to in legacy ABAP environments or ever older HANA environments, but am being told my SAP Support that this is perfectly normal behavior in my OSS request.

If it is then I cannot for the life or me understand how I missed seeing this for the last 20 years and would like some community input.

Issue:
have a role that has already been built via standard security process adding the Tcode to the role menu and then using the profile generator in export mode to "edit old status and merge with new" option.

When using that option now I am noticing that it is now taking "Active" standard authorization objects and if they match an inactive standard object it is merging the active ones into the inactive ones thus disabling the object and turning off the access for the user.

We only started noticing this when users started complaining that they were losing access in our productive environment and we went back to development and compared it to our sandbox system and noticed auth_object numbers were missing in development but were there in sandbox and the missing objects were now combined into one big disabled object.

so to correct this I used the "add authorization defaults" from the parent tree of the authorization values and added back the auth_objs and values for the Tcodes I wanted active and saved the role to turn them back on.
The tcode started working again with only the authorization objects allowed so that other access was not given inadvertently.

Then just to see if it was a fluke I used my expert mode again and to my surprise it again merged all of my active objects into inactive objects thus disabling the access again.

Finally just to see if the whole merge process worked like this i re-added my required objects which made them active, then selected the "merge" option under the authorization parent and ONLY the ACTIVE objects that shared standard activity values merged.  (this is what I expected and how it worked in legacy systems)

I saved the profile and backed out and then went back in and used "expert" mode and it then again combined all of my "Active" standard objects into the "Inactive" object, thus turning off access.

I tested this in a legacy system and it is not doing this using Expert mode there.


I opened up an OSS note and the reply from them was to link the document on how authorization object comparison works with the profile generator as the solution and them pretty much saying this is now working by design.

If this is now how the merge process works (taking active auths and combining them with inactive auths) then why would we every use the merge process in export mode every again and is there a way to disable/hide expert mode in the profile generator so that new security people do not accidentally disable productive access when it merges into a inactive object for some reason?

how do we get around the 100 object limitation in a profile if our only 2 options are to activate ALL similar Standard values (thus potentially granting access not required by the user for objects that are shared by the different Tcodes) or leaving the standard objects deactivated and then having to Manually add the ojects (big no no) OR go into SU24 and change SAP Standard values which would increase security work during upgrades and our SU25 process?


Thoughts?    Anyone else seeing this issue (or in SAP's response) Non-issue?

entry.title: Re: Saving sorting preferences for list of objects
entry.content: Yes, the filters will persist until the user specifically chooses to Reset them on the Filter page for the particular list.

entry.title: Re: Start with SAP BTP Document Management Service (DMS)
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Great blog!

However, I'm having issues while setting up a repository. I'm using a free tier account and when I try to create a repository I'm getting this error:

"Error in OnBoarding Repository: SubAccount needs to buy SDM-repository entitlement "

It's my understanding that the free account allows for up to 1 GB of storage - so not sure what I am missing.

Has anyone experienced this issue?

Thank you

entry.title: When it comes to leveraging AI in Supply Chain – The future is NOW!
entry.content:
“In the realm of ‘AI Everywhere,’ generative AI emerges as a transformative force, potentially revolutionizing the future”.

This was a major finding of a new IDC InfoBrief titled “The Importance of AI in supply chain and operations”.

However, the report also raised concerns around data bias and privacy with three major questions emerging:

  • Is my data in good enough shape?
  • Should I be a leader, early adopter, or fast follower?
  • Should AI be part of my analytics layer, or embedded within functional applications?

The importance of secure data

“Garbage In, Garbage Out”.

This has been a mantra of every implementation of software systems since Charles Babbage invented the first automatic computer. Although I am sure he wouldn’t have used the (very American) term “garbage”, but I am sure he would have agreed that if your data is not accurate, the results and recommendations that are generated will be wrong. 

As the IDC report points out “at the heart of successful AI implementations is the data”. But AI is only as good as the data it is analyzing. Both from the perspective of the quality of the data, and the availability of data to train the models. IDC suggest a 5-stage data maturity model that is covered in the report.

Data privacy is also a major area concern of supply chain respondents who lead the way with 93% rating the importance of data security and privacy in AI-powered ERP software a top concern compared to 8% of their peers in other lines of business.

When it comes to AI, should I lead or follow?

When it comes to AI, IDC points out “the future is NOW”.  

The advice from IDC is clear:

  • Begin to explore how AI tools can help with productivity and performance across your supply chain without delay.
  • Identify internal roles for AI innovation and transformation.
  • Implement AI data governance standards to reduce potential biases (e.g., training data diversity) and improve data privacy.

 

Creating a business strategy to leverage AI?

Business AI leveraging machine learning and natural language processing, analyses this data to generate insights and automate tasks.

This powerful combination of Cloud ERP and supply chain data and Business AI empowers businesses to transform operations, gain deeper insights, and make data-driven decisions with unprecedented accuracy.

This underscores the necessity for businesses to thoroughly assess and comprehend various related aspects to craft a robust AI Business Strategy. The key is to identify your business challenges you need to address, the use cases that will improve your business, and then determine how best to address them. 

The possible applications for AI in the business context seem endless, but how do you turn this potential into tangible value? What scenarios will work best for your business? How do you build an AI strategy?  And how will AI align with your specific operational requirements?

IDC defines a use case as “a technology-enabled, business-funded initiative that delivers a measurable outcome”. In the InfoBrief several use cases are highlighted including:

Productivity Use Cases

Supply chain practitioners live in the real-world and need information in real-time, and as the IDC Info Brief explains “need to be able to quickly identify and assess the impact of data through self-service knowledge discovery”, which came out as the highest priority in the IDC research.

This calls for a generative AI copilot such as SAP’s Joule that is embedded into business processes across supply chain and other lines of business. Joule helps people get work done faster and drive better business outcomes in a secure, compliant way. 

Data visualization and modeling and Intelligent summarization came in joint second to “help supply chains and operations make sense of data quickly and in a people-intuitive way”.  Making data-driven decisions in real time means better performance and reduced decision risk.

Business Function Use Cases

  • Dynamic Demand Forecasting and Inventory to better derive forecasts, inventory plans and strategies.
  • Supply Chain Orchestration to support the integration of information across supply chain silos.
  • Automated Inventory Replenishment by analyzing sales data, market trends, and other external factors.
  • Automated Customs Clearance by generating and managing customs documents and expediting the customs process.

 

To learn more about the importance of AI in Supply Chain Management click here to download the IDC InfoBrief.

And remember when it comes to AI, “the future is NOW”.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to Career Corner
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m excited to join the SAP community! As a Planner EAM (Enterprise Asset Management), I’ve been exposed to SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) and Materials Management (MM). However, I believe there’s much more to explore, and I’m eager to deepen my understanding of these modules. My ultimate goal is to become an SAP consultant. Here’s my plan:

Understanding the Modules
SAP Plant Maintenance (PM)
PM focuses on managing equipment, maintenance planning, and work orders.
I’ve already scratched the surface, but I want to delve deeper into PM processes and best practices.
Any advice or real-world scenarios related to PM would be greatly appreciated.

Materials Management (MM)
MM deals with procurement, inventory management, and material master data.
I’d like to explore MM thoroughly, especially its integration with other SAP modules.
Are there specific MM topics I should focus on?

I’m thrilled to embark on this SAP journey, and I’d love to hear your experiences and insights. Let’s learn and grow together!
